message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Melvyn's Restaurant (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~968 reviews; Tripadvisor ~976 reviews; OpenTable 4000+ reviews (combined: 5000+)
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: 50 years (opened 1975 by Mel Haber); celebrated 50th anniversary Oct-Nov 2025; located at Ingleside Estate (Ingleside Inn) which has operated as a hotel property for decades
- Credentials: ["Wine Spectator Award (2023, per restaurant site)","Featured in Esquire's 'Best Bars in America' (per restaurant site) [UNVERIFIED year]","Featured in The Infatuation LA's '25 Best Places To Eat & Drink in Palm Springs'","Featured in Netflix documentary 'Frank Sinatra in Palm Springs'","OpenTable Diners' Choice listings (romantic / live music / fine dining categories)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'classic Old Hollywood restaurants Coachella Valley', 'Sinatra steak Diane Palm Springs', 'supper club Palm Springs', 'date night Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Springs'. Less consistent for generic 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' where newer/El Paseo venues dominate.
- Site structured data: Website (inglesideestate.com/melvyns/) presents Melvyn's as a sub-page of the parent estate site rather than a standalone restaurant property. Hours, address, phone, and OpenTable reservation link are present and clean, but no visible JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant or LocalBusiness markup was detected. No explicit cuisine type, price range, or service-type schema. Menu is reachable but not structured for agents. Strong narrative content and imagery; weak technical SEO for AI ingestion.
- Visibility gap score: 5
- Convertibility tier: unclear

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Discover the Old Hollywood Allure of Melvyn’s | ICONIC LIFE","url":"https://iconiclife.com/behind-the-iconic-martinis-melvyns-at-the-ingleside-estate/"},{"title":"Mel Haber - Wikipedia","url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mel_Haber"},{"title":"Frank Sinatra's Palm Springs Visits: Behind the Scenes with Mel Haber","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/frank-sinatra-remember-by-mel-haber-at-melvyns-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Step Back in Time: Palm Springs Melvyn’s at the Ingleside Inn","url":"https://www.milesgeek.com/melvyns-ingleside-inn/"},{"title":"Mel Haber, Owner of a Legendary Celebrity Hotspot in Palm Springs, Dies at 80","url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/lifestyle/lifestyle-news/mel-haber-dead-palm-springs-restaurateur-was-80-941836/"}]

Based on the search results, here's the information about Melvyn's Restaurant Palm Springs:

## Melvyn's Restaurant - 50th Anniversary Celebration

Melvyn's opened its doors in 1975 at the historic Ingleside Estate, under the vision of Mel Haber. Over five weeks, each era, from the restaurant's founding in 1975 through 2025, was honored with era-inspired menus, classic cocktails, period music, and evocative moments that showcased how Melvyn's evolved while still staying true to its roots.

The celebration culminated with a grand anniversary event on November 15, 2025, featuring live music, nostalgic entertainment, and an evening of open-bar cocktails and bites inspired by Melvyn's original menu.

## The Ingleside Estate

The Ingleside Estate still exudes the charm of its 1920s architecture, with lush courtyards, arched corridors, and intimate dining rooms that feel untouched by time. When Mel Haber arrived in Palm Springs in 1975, he discovered a neglected estate on the edge of downtown—the Ingleside Inn, a Spanish Colonial Revival retreat dating back to 1922. Originally built as a private estate for the Birge family of the Pierce-Arrow Motor Car Company, it had once been an exclusive hideaway for the elite under Ruth Hardy's ownership.

## Legacy and Significance

The iconic restaurant once favored by Hollywood greats like Marlon Brando, Frank Sinatra, Marilyn Monroe, and Elizabeth Taylor, is celebrating its 50th anniversary.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Melvyn's Palm Springs reviews across multiple platforms:

## OpenTable Reviews

Melvyn's at Ingleside Estate is a Palm Springs classic, adored for its "exquisite mid-century vibe" and "top notch service and food." Guests rave about the "perfectly cooked filet" and "delicious" table-side dishes like Steak Diane, with the ambiance featuring "live piano music" adding to the nostalgic charm.

## Yelp Reviews

Located on the premises of the legendary Ingleside Estate hotel, Melvyn's is elegantly upbeat and the home of authentic Hollywood glamour.

## TripAdvisor Reviews

TripAdvisor reviews are mixed. One positive review described booking the enclosed patio for a milestone birthday with 50 people, with managers and staff making the event seamless, and guests particularly enjoying the Steak Diane table-side presentation. However, there are also negative reviews, with some guests criticizing service quality, portion sizes, and pricing.

## Overall Sentiment

The staff is described as friendly, with new management doing a great job of improving this old Palm Springs favorite restaurant, with the atmosphere enhanced by live piano music. The restaurant is particularly praised for its historic ambiance and special occasions dining, though experiences appear to vary.

Links: [{"title":"Melvyn's Restaurant & Bar - Palm Springs | Ingleside Estate","url":"https://inglesideestate.com/melvyns-50th-anniversary/"},{"title":"Melvyn's Restaurant | Vacation Palm Springs","url":"https://vacationpalmsprings.com/restaurant/melvyns-restaurant"},{"title":"Melvyn’s Palm Springs Celebrates 50 Years of Glamour","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/melvyns-at-50-a-toast-to-glamour-legacy-and-the-man-who-made-palm-springs-shine/"},{"title":"goop | Melvyn’s","url":"https://goop.com/place/california/palm-springs/historic-tennis-club-restaurants/melvyns/"},{"title":"Looking for Somewhere to Dine During Modernism Week? Discover the Old Hollywood Allure of Melvyn’s | ICONIC LIFE","url":"https://iconiclife.com/behind-the-iconic-martinis-melvyns-at-the-ingleside-estate/"},{"title":"The Desert Lounge Where Old Hollywood Lives - PUNCH","url":"https://punchdrink.com/articles/the-desert-lounge-where-old-hollywood-lives/"},{"title":"Jesse Bluma at Pointe Viven: Your Next Lesson in Gourmet Dining: Melvyn’s Restaurant Review","url":"http://pointeviven.blogspot.com/2017/07/Your-Next-Lesson-in-Gourmet-Dining-Melvyns-Restaurant-Casablanca-Lounge-Bar-Jesse-Bluma-Pointe-Viven-Palm-Springs-Review-Zomato-Number-1-One-Ruth-Hardy-Mel-Haber.html"},{"title":"Palm Springs Rat Pack Hangout Melvyn’s Gets “Cool” New Look","url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/palm-springs-rat-pack-hangout-melvyns-gets-cool-new-look-1068764/"}]

Here's what I found about Melvyn's in Palm Springs:

## Overview
Melvyn's is a classic supper club-style establishment set in a romantic and nostalgic inn located just west of downtown Palm Springs. Its classic supper-club ambiance features chandeliers, velvet seating, live music, and tableside service.

## Connection to Frank Sinatra
Icons such as Frank Sinatra, Marlon Brando, Marilyn Monroe, Elizabeth Taylor, and members of the Rat Pack frequented the restaurant. Frank Sinatra became one of its most loyal patrons, making Table 13 his own, and he famously held his rehearsal dinner there in 1976 before marrying Barbara Marx.

## The Classic Supper Club Experience
The menu is reminiscent of New York in the 1930s: Shrimp Cocktails, Oysters Rockerfeller, Steak au Poive, French Chicken Breast, and countless other tributes to the past. The restaurant insists waiters wear linen jackets and hosts live music and dancing nightly at the adjoining piano lounge.

## Historic Setting
The property dates back to 1922, when it was built by the Pierce-Arrow family, and was originally a single-family home that reflected the prestige and elegance of its era. The estate was revived in 1975 by Mel Haber, a New York businessman and inventor best known for creating fuzzy dice, who transformed the former service area into Melvyn's Restaurant.

content.content:
# Melvyn's Tripadvisor Reviews Summary

**Total Reviews:** 976

**Rating:** 4.1 out of 5 bubbles

**Overall Sentiment:** Mixed to negative. While the restaurant has a storied reputation and nostalgic appeal, recent reviews reveal significant concerns. The AI-generated summary notes that guests praise "friendly and attentive waitstaff" but criticize "inattentive and disorganized service." Food quality receives mixed feedback, with complaints about "bland flavors and small portions," though some "standout dishes and classic desserts" earn praise. The atmosphere is described as "dated," and value is consistently criticized as "pricey," with customers noting "additional fees adding to the cost."

Based on my web search, here's what I found about Melvyn's in Palm Springs:

## Melvyn's at Ingleside Estate - Palm Springs

Located on the premises of the legendary Ingleside Estate hotel, Melvyn's is elegantly upbeat and the home of authentic Hollywood glamour.

### Recognition and Awards
Melvyn's is a classic, romantic pick for date night, with guests frequently praising the old-school Hollywood glamour, live piano music in the bar, table-side Steak Diane and flambé desserts (Bananas Foster, Cherries Jubilee).

### Dining Experience
Melvyn's at Ingleside Estate is a Palm Springs classic, adored for its "exquisite mid-century vibe" and "top notch service and food." Melvyn's serves refined takes on classic American fare, including steaks, fresh seafood, elegant pastas, and nostalgic sides.

### Atmosphere
The lounge at Melvyn's offers a vintage-inspired setting with a full bar menu, seasonal cocktails, and nightly live piano.

content.text: I have enough verification to call this out.

confirms: Yelp ~968 reviews — confirmed via Yelp search snippet (570 Photos & 968 Reviews)
Tripadvisor ~976 reviews — confirmed directly from Tripadvisor page (976, 4.1/5)
Longevity: opened 1975 by Mel Haber at Ingleside Estate — confirmed by visitpalmsprings.com, pstribune.com, Hollywood Reporter, Wikipedia
50th anniversary celebrated 2025 with 'Dining Through the Decades' series culminating Nov 15, 2025 — confirmed by pstribune.com and spaandbeautytoday.com
Ingleside Estate property dates to 1922 (Birge/Pierce-Arrow); operated as inn for decades — confirmed

Wine Spectator Award (2023), Esquire 'Best Bars in America', Infatuation LA '25 Best Places To Eat & Drink in Palm Springs' — all confirmed present on restaurant's own site (inglesideestate.com/melvyns/)

Site structured-data assessment: no JSON-LD/schema.org Restaurant markup detected on inglesideestate.com/melvyns/; hours, address (200 W Ramon Rd), phone (760-325-2323), and OpenTable link are clean and present — confirmed
Surfaces strongly for 'classic Old Hollywood', 'Sinatra/supper club Palm Springs', 'date night' queries — confirmed via PUNCH, ICONIC LIFE, goop, Hollywood Reporter coverage

Less consistent for generic 'best fine dining Palm Springs/Coachella Valley' — confirmed: OpenTable, Palm Springs Life, Cavallo Ranch, AvantStay 'best fine dining' lists in my searches surfaced Le Vallauris, Wally's Desert Turtle, Copley's, 4 Saints, Edge Steakhouse, Miro's, Purple Room — Melvyn's did NOT appear in the top generic fine-dining roundups returned
Sentiment 'mostly positive' — partially confirmed (Yelp/OpenTable lean positive; Tripadvisor explicitly 'mixed to negative' per AI summary on page)
contradicts: Sentiment claim 'mostly positive' is overstated on Tripadvisor specifically — Tripadvisor's own AI-generated summary flags 'inattentive and disorganized service', 'bland flavors and small portions', 'dated' atmosphere, and pricey value. Rating is only 4.1/5. Yelp/OpenTable skew more positive but Tripadvisor sentiment is mixed-to-negative, not mostly positive.
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable review count '4000+' — could not independently confirm; OpenTable page timed out and search results did not surface a specific count. Combined 5000+ total is therefore unverified.
Esquire 'Best Bars in America' specific year — restaurant lists it as 'Twenty-Seven Best Bars in America' but no year confirmed independently

Netflix documentary 'Frank Sinatra in Palm Springs' — could not independently verify Melvyn's is featured in a Netflix documentary by this exact title (Sinatra/Palm Springs/Melvyn's heritage is well-documented, but the specific Netflix doc credit was not surfaced in my searches)

OpenTable Diners' Choice category badges (romantic / live music / fine dining) for Melvyn's specifically — Diners' Choice category lists exist on OpenTable but I could not verify Melvyn's current presence on each named sub-category
Convertibility tier 'unclear' — not a factual claim to verify
input.notes: Core identity, longevity, 50th anniversary, Yelp/Tripadvisor counts, and structured-data weakness all hold up cleanly. Two real issues: (1) sentiment is more uneven than 'mostly positive' suggests — Tripadvisor's own summary is mixed-to-negative on service/value, which an honest assessment should reflect; (2) OpenTable 4000+ count and the Netflix doc credit are not independently verifiable from public search. Visibility-gap score of 5 (mid-range) is reasonable: strong narrative/editorial presence + clean NAP, but absent schema.org markup and weak surfacing in generic 'best fine dining' lists where El Paseo and newer venues dominate, exactly as claimed.
